narrative_text: The leaders of the Quraysh gathered in secret at Dar al-Nadwah, their meeting hall. They were angry and determined - they had decided that the Prophet Muhammad must be stopped, no matter the cost.

Abu Jahl stood up with a wicked plan. 'Each clan should choose a strong young warrior,' he said. 'Together, they will attack Muhammad at his door tonight. The blame will be shared, so no one can seek revenge!' The other leaders nodded in agreement.

section_heading: The Prophet Goes to Abu Bakr
narrative_text: The Prophet quickly went to the home of his dearest friend, Abu Bakr. With urgency in his step, he came to share the news that Allah had finally given them permission to leave Makkah.

section_heading: The Prophet Goes to Abu Bakr
narrative_text: When the Prophet told him, 'Abu Bakr, you will be my companion,' Abu Bakr wept tears of joy. He had been waiting for this day. He had already prepared two strong camels, feeding them carefully for many months!
Asma worked quickly to prepare food for the long dangerous journey. She gathered rounds of flat bread, sweet dates, and dried meat, ready to be packed.
But she had no rope to tie the bundles tight! Without a moment's hesitation, Asma took her own belt from her waist and tore it in two with her bare hands.
She used one half of her belt for each bundle. From that day forward, Asma was honored with a beautiful title: 'Dhat al-Nitaqayn' - 'She of the Two Belts.'
Brave young Ali volunteered for a dangerous task. He would sleep in the Prophet's bed, wearing his green cloak, to trick the enemies. He did not fear danger when serving the Prophet.
Outside, the assassins waited eagerly with their swords drawn. They thought their plan was perfect. They watched the door of the Prophet's house, ready to strike at any moment.

The Prophet quietly recited verses from the Quran and walked right out of his house, past all the assassins! Allah miraculously protected him - the assassins could not see him at all. He even scattered a handful of dust over them and walked safely away.

section_heading: Hiding in the Cave of Thawr
narrative_text: The Prophet and Abu Bakr hid deep inside the Cave of Thawr in the rocky mountains south of Makkah. Outside, the Quraysh searched everywhere for them, even offering a great reward.

section_heading: Hiding in the Cave of Thawr
narrative_text: The pursuers came so close! They reached the very entrance of the cave! Their voices echoed as they searched, looking for any sign of the travelers.

section_heading: Hiding in the Cave of Thawr
narrative_text: Abu Bakr was so afraid! He whispered, 'If they look down, they will see us!' The Prophet smiled calmly and said with perfect trust: 'Do not be sad - Allah is with us.' His words filled Abu Bakr's heart with peace.

After the long journey, the people of Madinah saw them coming! Songs of joy filled the air as men, women, and children rushed out to welcome the beloved Prophet. They had been waiting for this moment for so long.
Every clan in Madinah wanted the Prophet to stay with them, each holding the rein of his camel. But the Prophet kindly said, 'Let her go - she has her orders.' He let his camel choose where to stop.

The camel walked through the streets and finally stopped right in front of the home of Abu Ayyub! He could hardly believe his good fortune as he ran out, ready to welcome the Prophet of Allah into his humble home.
